era: Hip-Hop. E=6 -- classic boom-bap production revival as artistic identity. Self-released. Underground circuit. Twenty-eighth degree in hip-hop era (134-161). E=6 zone opens (161-172). v1.49.301.

location.formative: Seattle underground hip-hop scene. Beat-making collectives, crate-digging communities, record stores.
location.career: Boom Bap Project. Classic boom-bap production. Self-released. Small venues. Direct-to-fan distribution.

associated_musicians.name: 206 Zulu (degree 142)
associated_musicians.note: Hip-hop cultural bedrock. Boom-bap production is one of the foundational elements 206 Zulu maintains.

associated_musicians.name: Blue Scholars (degree 156)
associated_musicians.note: Sabzi's boom-bap production for Blue Scholars. Shared production tradition.

theory_nodes.concept: Production as identity -- the group name IS the production style. Boom-bap is not a choice but the identity itself. Dedication to a single foundational production style as the artistic statement.
examples: The name 'Boom Bap Project' declares the mission.
Classic crisp snare, deep kick, sample-driven loops.
Revival as living practice, not nostalgia.
theory_nodes.description: Production identity. Boom-bap as the art form itself.

ancestors: DJ Premier, Pete Rock, Large Professor, Q-Tip.
genealogy.relationship: Boom Bap Project at 161. Production as identity.

theory_nodes.concept: Classic revival -- late 1980s/early 1990s production revived in Seattle. New music within a foundational framework. The difference between revivalism (makes new things) and nostalgia (replays old things).
examples: Boom-bap: crisp snare + deep kick + sample loops.
Sample selection as art: the right two bars of a forgotten record.
theory_nodes.description: Classic boom-bap revival. Foundational sound kept alive.

genealogy.relationship: Boom Bap Project at 161. Revival, not nostalgia.

theory_nodes.concept: E=6 zone opens -- degrees 161-172 sustained E=6 zone. Nearly all hip-hop entries at maximum energy. Boom Bap Project sets the production-conscious tone.
theory_nodes.description: E=6 zone opens. Sustained high energy in hip-hop era.

genealogy.relationship: Boom Bap Project at 161. E=6 zone opens.

cross_references.relationship: PRODUCTION CONTRAST! Lewis: horn-driven, anthemic, mainstream. Boom Bap Project: sample-driven, underground, foundational. Adjacent degrees, different production philosophies.
BOOM-BAP LINEAGE! Sabzi's boom-bap for Blue Scholars. Boom Bap Project continues the tradition. Shared production roots.
CULTURAL CONNECTION! The foundational collective. Boom-bap is one of the elements 206 Zulu maintains. Production style connects to cultural practice.
